channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pope Leo XIV’s First Year: Balancing Reform, Social Teaching and Global Challenges
item.description: Pope Leo XIV, the first US-born pontiff and former Augustinian missionary in Peru, has spent his first year navigating the complexities of leading a global Catholic Church. From the outset, his focus ...

item.title: Gachagua Addresses Police, Promises Independence Under His Presidency
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua addressed police officers outside Milimani Law Courts, reiterating his belief that the National Police Service is under undue political influence. Speaking con...

item.title: London Grand Theatre Concludes Season with Heartfelt Production of ‘Come From Away’
item.description: The London Grand Theatre is closing its season with the acclaimed musical 'Come From Away,' a production celebrated for its true story and inspirational themes. The show recounts the remarkable events...

item.title: Legal experts weigh in on death penalty calls after Monswamy family murders
item.description: Following the tragic murder of the Monswamy family in KwaZulu-Natal's north coast, South Africans have taken to social media demanding the return of the death penalty. The victims included Alan Monswa...

item.title: Reports claim US passports may feature former President Trump’s image
item.description: The Sky News article titled 'US to issue passports featuring Trump's image' could not be accessed due to server restrictions, leaving the full details unavailable. Based on the URL and title, it appea...

item.title: King Charles III Addresses U.S. Congress on 250th Anniversary of American Independence
item.description: King Charles III addressed a joint session of the U.S. Congress during his four-day state visit to mark the 250th anniversary of American independence. Only the second British monarch to speak in Cong...

item.title: UK summons Iranian ambassador following inflammatory embassy social media posts
item.description: The United Kingdom’s Foreign Office has summoned Iran’s ambassador, Seyed Ali Mousavi, after the Iranian embassy in London posted social media messages that were described as 'unacceptable and inflamm...

item.title: TikTok Users Enter Scientology Buildings in 'Speed Running' Trend, Church Calls for Police Action
item.description: In Los Angeles, a TikTok trend known as 'speed running' has seen teenagers sprinting through multiple Church of Scientology buildings on Hollywood Boulevard. Videos showing people dressed as character...

item.title: The Risks and Security Challenges of Facial Recognition Data
item.description: Facial recognition systems are increasingly used in public spaces, retail, airports, and office buildings, converting faces into mathematical templates rather than storing actual photos. While templat...

item.title: Siphosethu Mvula Set to Face Bandile Daniels in Mdantsane Showdown
item.description: Boxing manager Andile “Black Buffalo” Mshumpela has announced that former IBF Africa junior featherweight champion Siphosethu “Rain” Mvula will return to the ring against top-ranked contender Bandile ...

item.title: Cherargei Proposes VIP Mental Health Unit for Political Leaders at Mathari Hospital
item.description: Nandi Senator Samson Cherargei has proposed setting up a dedicated VIP mental health unit at Mathari National Teaching and Referral Hospital to cater specifically for political leaders. Speaking in th...

item.title: King Charles Calls For Stronger US-UK Cooperation Amid Global Challenges
item.description: King Charles III waka go US Congress give speech wey rare, dey urge America make e stand gidigba with him Western allies as wahala for Iran and Ukraine dey shake transatlantic relations. The monarch s...

item.title: Chimpanzees adjust nest-building based on expected overnight weather
item.description: Researchers studying eastern chimpanzees in Nyungwe National Park, Rwanda, have discovered that these primates exhibit remarkable foresight when it comes to nest construction. Over a 12-month field st...

item.title: Cape Town Faces Massive Housing Backlog with Hundreds of Thousands Waiting
item.description: Cape Town is grappling with a significant housing crisis, with approximately 440 000 applicants registered on the City’s Housing Needs Register. The City has earmarked nearly R2.8 billion for human se...

item.title: Kenyan Family Appeals for Support to Repatriate Son's Body After US Motorcycle Accident
item.description: The Morema family in Nyamira County, Kenya, is mourning yet again after losing their only surviving son, Joseph Morema, in a tragic motorcycle accident in the United States. Joseph, 29, passed away on...

item.title: Morgan McSweeney testifies on Mandelson appointment without harming Starmer
item.description: Morgan McSweeney, former chief of staff to Keir Starmer, appeared before the Foreign Affairs Select Committee to discuss his role in recommending Peter Mandelson as US ambassador. The session, led by ...

item.title: Donald Trump recalls his mother admired King Charles during White House visit
item.description: During the recent state visit of the British royals to the White House, former US President Donald Trump shared a personal anecdote about his mother, Mary Anne MacLeod. Trump revealed that his mother ...

item.title: Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni sentenced to life in prison for 2016 murder of BJP leader Yogeshgouda Goudar
item.description: In a significant verdict, a Bengaluru court has sentenced Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni to life imprisonment for the murder of Bharatiya Janata Party leader Yogeshgouda Goudar, which occurred in June 20...

item.title: ABS-CBN airs the comeback of “Everybody Sing” hosted by Vice Ganda on ALLTV
item.description: ABS-CBN, one of the leading media and entertainment companies in the Philippines, has announced the return of its popular musical game show “Everybody Sing.” This time, the show will be hosted by the ...

item.title: Former FBI Director James Comey Indicted Over Instagram Seashell Photo Interpreted as Threat to President Trump
item.description: Former FBI Director James Comey was indicted on April 28, 2026, in the Eastern District of North Carolina on charges of making a threat against the president and transmitting a threat in interstate co...
